name: NLTK (Natural Language Toolkit)

performance.notes: CPU-based rule-based processing; fast for tokenization/tagging

agent_readiness.notes: Classic NLP toolkit for educational and preprocessing tasks. word_tokenize(text). pos_tag(tokens). ne_chunk() (named entity). FreqDist(). concordance(). WordNet: wn.synsets('dog'). Stemming: PorterStemmer(), LancasterStemmer(). Lemmatization: WordNetLemmatizer(). Corpora: reuters, brown, gutenberg. VADER sentiment analysis. stopwords.words('english'). Classic education NLP. Still widely used for preprocessing, stopwords, tokenization.

gotchas: nltk.download() required for each corpus/resource (one-time setup)
Not suitable for production-scale NLP (spaCy/transformers faster)
POS tagger less accurate than neural alternatives
Old-school rule-based approach; poor for modern deep learning workflows
best_when: NLP education and learning core concepts
WordNet semantic relationships and lexical resources
VADER sentiment analysis for social media text
avoid_when: Production NLP pipelines (use spaCy or transformers)
Modern ML-based NLP tasks (POS, NER, parsing)
summary: NLTK is the classic Python NLP toolkit, primarily used for education and preprocessing. word_tokenize(), pos_tag(), ne_chunk(), WordNet, FreqDist, VADER sentiment. stopwords, stemming, lemmatization. 50+ corpora. Still widely used for preprocessing pipelines (stopwords removal, tokenization). Use spaCy for production NLP; use NLTK for learning NLP concepts and WordNet access.
